scored.recoheredEarlier: I keep trying to read it like it says something about all the rows.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue expressing the field/value distinction with decreasing need to pause, moving toward fluent application.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Field `month`, value `April`. I think `month` is the column name, and `April` is the cell for this row.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: L4 completes the tutor's blank-fill task with correct classification and adds a synonymous self-explanation, showing the learner can now perform the header/value pairing independently.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner turn creates pressure disrupting the tutor's approach; the tutor consistently refines the same distinction without breaking a prior habit or introducing a new mechanism.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No later tutor turn exists after the pivot to respond to any revised learner framing.

scored.naiveTrajectory: Learner will place the rubbing card with the correct direction as instructed.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Rubbing is table on cart, rubbing; the arrow goes opposite the motion strip, along the track. Is that direction right?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: L3 performs the requested labeling and arrow placement but does not enact a new device or criterion; it is a correct, prompted performance.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner turn creates pressure for a reversal, and the tutor continues with the same instructional pattern.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn and no subsequent tutor uptake of a revised framing.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I still do not like that the typed row looks usable, but the mark belongs on the sorted strip.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to accept the bench check and copy the median number without reinterpreting the earlier complaint.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Two lower, two higher on the sorted strip. The capped-marker number is the median; the paperclip card stays out of the report. Fine, the extra sort is doing the check.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: L4 executes the whole median procedure—copying the marked number, verifying the two-low-two-high, and explicitly excluding the paperclip—demonstrating the completed device rather than just stating understanding.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Keep both marks visible. The paperclip shows why the typed row looked usable; the capped marker shows the median rule. Before it goes into the summary, do the bench check: name the marked number, then count the two below and two above it on the sorted strip.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: Tutor responds to L2's lingering resistance by keeping both marks visible and introducing the 'bench check,' which adds a new criterion (counting two below/two above) but retains the same sorted-strip representation and object, thus adapting without fully breaking the prior route.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: After L3's pivot, T3 simply confirms the check and continues the planned copying task without adapting to the learner's reframing of the paperclip as failing a test.
